concepts.title: Types of Queues
concepts.description: There are two types of queues: 1. Linear Queue 2. Circular Queue.

concepts.title: Queue Operations
concepts.description: The basic operations performed on a queue are: Enqueue (add an element), Dequeue (remove an element), Peek (view the front element), and isEmpty (check if the queue is empty).

concepts.title: Queue Implementation
concepts.description: A queue can be implemented using arrays or linked lists. The choice of implementation depends on the specific requirements of the application.

concepts.title: Enqueue and Dequeue
concepts.description: To remember the order of Enqueue and Dequeue operations: 'E' is the first letter of 'Enter' and 'D' is the first letter of 'Delete'.

concepts.title: Queue vs Stack
concepts.description: Students often confuse queues and stacks. A key difference is that a queue follows the FIFO (First-In-First-Out) principle, whereas a stack follows the LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) principle.

concepts.title: Queue Time Complexity
concepts.description: The time complexity for Enqueue operation is O(1) and for Dequeue operation is also O(1) for array implementation. However, for linked list implementation, the time complexity for both Enqueue and Dequeue operations is O(1).

concepts.title: Queue Applications
concepts.description: Queues have numerous real-world applications, including job scheduling, print queues, and network protocols.

concepts.title: Queue Data Structure
concepts.description: A queue is a linear data structure that follows the FIFO principle, where elements are added to the rear and removed from the front.
